heading: Energy efficiency materials pilot program
label: 18832 Energy efficiency materials pilot program
notes: References in TextThe Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in subsec. (a)(3), is classified generally to Title 26, Internal Revenue Code.

Wage Rate RequirementsFor provisions relating to rates of wages to be paid to laborers and mechanics on projects for construction, alteration, or repair work funded under div. D or an amendment by div. D of , including authority of Secretary of Labor, see .Pub. L. 117–58section 18851 of this title
amendments: Wage Rate RequirementsFor provisions relating to rates of wages to be paid to laborers and mechanics on projects for construction, alteration, or repair work funded under div. D or an amendment by div. D of , including authority of Secretary of Labor, see .Pub. L. 117–58section 18851 of this title

children.heading: Applicant
children.label: 1 Applicant
children.text: The term “applicant” means a nonprofit organization that applies for a grant under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “energy-efficiency material” means a material (including a product, equipment, or system) the installation of which results in a reduction in use by a nonprofit organization of energy or fuel.

children.label: i
children.text: a roof or lighting system or component of the system;

children.label: iii
children.text: a door, including a security door; and

children.label: iv
children.text: a heating, ventilation, or air conditioning system or component of the system (including insulation and wiring and plumbing improvements needed to serve a more efficient system).

children.heading: Nonprofit building
children.label: 3 Nonprofit building
children.text: The term “nonprofit building” means a building operated and owned by an organization that is described in section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and exempt from tax under section 501(a) of such Code.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: b Establishment
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall establish a pilot program to award grants for the purpose of providing nonprofit buildings with energy-efficiency materials.November 15, 2021

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may award grants under the program established under subsection (b).

children.heading: Application
children.label: 2 Application
children.text: The Secretary may award a grant under paragraph (1) if an applicant submits to the Secretary an application at such time, in such form, and containing such information as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.label: B
children.text: the cost effectiveness of the use of energy-efficiency materials;

children.label: C
children.text: an effective plan for evaluation, measurement, and verification of energy savings; and

children.label: D
children.text: the financial need of the applicant.

children.heading: Limitation on individual grant amount
children.label: 4 Limitation on individual grant amount
children.text: Each grant awarded under this section shall not exceed $200,000.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this section $50,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026, to remain available until expended.
